name: Augmentoolkit
tagline: Turn raw documents into synthetic datasets that train domain-expert LLMs
seoTitle: Augmentoolkit - Synthetic Data for Domain-Expert LLMs
metaDescription: Augmentoolkit turns your documents into synthetic instruction and conversation datasets for fine-tuning domain-expert LLMs. MIT-licensed, runs offline.
overview: Augmentoolkit is an open-source tool that takes your raw documents and generates synthetic instruction and conversation datasets you can use to fine-tune a language model. The goal is to teach a model the information inside your text so it becomes an expert in a chosen area, rather than relying only on retrieval at query time.

It is built for people who want a custom LLM trained on their own sources: researchers feeding in papers, hobbyists encoding the lore of a fictional universe, or teams building an internal expert. You upload documents, run a pipeline, and get a dataset (and optionally a trained model). It can also produce a RAG-ready dataset if you want traditional grounding alongside fine-tuning.

As a synthetic-data and labeling tool, Augmentoolkit can run offline on your own hardware with no external API key for data generation, or use an open-source LLM API to generate data faster. Pipelines are Python functions, so the workflow is meant to be forked and customized.
features: Converts uploaded documents into synthetic instruction and conversation datasets for fine-tuning
Optionally runs offline on your own machine with no external API key for data generation
Works with open-source models such as Deepseek and Llama, and ships its own dataset generation model
Can produce a RAG-ready dataset and start an inference server for traditional grounding
Offers both a guided interface and a CLI, with platform setup scripts for macOS and Linux
Pipelines are plain Python functions, so you can fork and build custom dataset generators

gettingStarted.intro: Augmentoolkit is installed by cloning the repo and running the setup script for your platform with Python 3.11. The interface then guides you through generating your first dataset.

steps.heading: Install on macOS
steps.body: Clone the repo and run the macOS setup script. It will attempt to install Valkey via brew if it isn't found. Use local_macos.sh instead if you plan to generate datasets locally.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/e-p-armstrong/augmentoolkit.git
cd augmentoolkit # Python == 3.11
bash macos.sh

steps.heading: Install on Linux
steps.body: Clone the repo and run the Linux setup script. It builds Valkey from source if no Redis/Valkey server is already running.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/e-p-armstrong/augmentoolkit.git
cd augmentoolkit # Python == 3.11
bash linux.sh

steps.heading: Run local inference on Linux
steps.body: To generate datasets locally, use the local script. Pass "small" or a custom model name to serve a quantized model for consumer hardware, and add --tensor-parallelism N if you have multiple GPUs.

steps.heading: Windows
steps.body: On Windows the recommended path is to run Augmentoolkit under WSL, or to use the CLI, which the project notes is easier to get running on Windows.

useCases: Fine-tune a model on the latest research papers so it answers as an expert in your field
Build an internal LLM that has learned your team's documents and reference material
Create a lore expert trained on the sources of a niche or fictional universe
Generate a RAG-ready dataset and spin up an inference server for grounded answers
